Abnormal to See: Multiple failed attempts followed by success from same IP; login from unfamiliar external IP
Normal to See: Occasional mistyped passwords from known IPs
What Value It Gives About Initial Access: Indicates brute force or unauthorized remote SSH access

Abnormal to See: Successful login from unknown public IP
First-time login from foreign country or unusual ASN
High volume of failures from one IP
What Value It Gives About Initial Access: Shows brute force or credential stuffing attempt

Normal to See: Root login disabled or internal only

What Value It Gives About Initial Access: Shows key-based entry instead of password

Abnormal to See: Password changed shortly before suspicious login
What Value It Gives About Initial Access: May indicate credential compromise or takeover

Artifact: Sudo usage immediately after first login
Abnormal to See: First login followed immediately by sudo attempts
Requests to /wp-admin, /phpmyadmin, long encoded strings, scanning patterns
What Value It Gives About Initial Access: Indicates web vulnerability scanning or exploitation

Abnormal to See: Repeated 404 errors, suspicious user agents, automated scanning patterns
What Value It Gives About Initial Access: Shows automated reconnaissance and exploit attempts

Abnormal to See: Anonymous login or unexpected upload activity
Normal to See: Anonymous FTP disabled and no uploads
Services bound only to internal interfaces

Abnormal to See: Outdated SSH, Apache, or exposed services
Segfault or crash prior to suspicious login
Suspicious spoofed sender or malicious attachment delivery
